content.speakerText: I'd like to leave by the end of next month—would that work?
content.speakerTextCn: 我想下月底离职，这样可以吗？

options.text: That sounds great! I'll start training your replacement tomorrow.
Actually, I need you to leave today.
Why not wait until next year?
I don't care when you leave.

content.explanation: 正确答案体现了积极回应并支持交接安排，符合职场协商离职的礼貌与专业性。选项2过于强硬，不符合协商语境；选项3回避问题且不切实际；选项4态度冷漠，缺乏职业素养。

content.speakerText: I know two weeks is standard, but I'm happy to stay longer if needed.
content.speakerTextCn: 我知道两周是惯例，但需要我多留点时间我愿意。

options.text: You're being too nice, just go on time.
I'm not sure we can afford to pay you longer.
Great! Then stay for three months.

content.explanation: 正确答案是对对方职业态度的真诚认可，符合职场沟通中的正向反馈原则。选项2虽真实但语气生硬，缺乏同理心；选项3涉及敏感财务话题，不适宜在协商中提出；选项4直接要求延长，违背了对方主动让步的善意。

content.speakerText: I'm planning to wrap up by the end of this week. Is that too soon?
content.speakerTextCn: 我计划这周末前结束工作，这会不会太早了？

options.text: That works for us—we can finish the handover by Friday.
You can't leave yet, we're too busy.
I guess you could go, but I'm not happy about it.
Why don't you just quit without notice?

content.explanation: 正确答案以积极态度确认离职时间并承诺交接，体现专业性。选项2直接拒绝且无建设性；选项3表达负面情绪，破坏合作氛围；选项4建议不辞而别，严重违反职场规范。

content.template: I'd like to leave by the end of next month—would that ___?
content.hint: 提出离职日期时的常用句型
content.knowledgePoint: would that work（协商类核心句型）

content.template: I know two weeks is standard, but I'm happy to stay ___ if needed.
content.hint: 表达愿意延长离职时间的常用词
content.knowledgePoint: stay longer（主动让步的核心搭配）

content.template: We need ___ time to complete the transition smoothly.
content.hint: 确认离职时间是否合理时的常用表达
content.knowledgePoint: enough time（交接流程的关键表达）

content.speakerText: I'd like to leave by the end of next month—would that work?
content.speakerTextCn: 我想下月底离职，这样可以吗？
expectedAnswers: That gives us enough time to transition.
End of next month works perfectly.
Could you consider leaving two weeks earlier so we can start the handover sooner?
evaluationCriteria: 意图达成度：是否明确回应了离职日期的可行性
语言准确性：语法和词汇使用是否正确
表达自然度：是否符合职场协商的礼貌语气
关键词覆盖：是否使用了 transition / handover / end of month 等关键词

content.speakerText: I know two weeks is standard, but I'm happy to stay longer if needed.
content.speakerTextCn: 我知道两周是惯例，但需要我多留点时间我愿意。
expectedAnswers: Thanks—I'll let you know if we need more.
We really appreciate your flexibility; two weeks will be sufficient.
evaluationCriteria: 意图达成度：是否对对方的让步表达了认可或回应
语言准确性：是否正确使用了 thoughtfully / appreciate / flexibility 等高级词汇
表达自然度：语气是否真诚、专业，避免过度承诺或冷漠
关键词覆盖：是否使用了 thoughtful / flexibility / sufficient 等职场关键词

content.speakerText: I'm aiming to wrap up by the end of this week. Do you think that’s feasible?
content.speakerTextCn: 我计划这周末前结束工作，你觉得可行吗？
expectedAnswers: That works—we can finalize everything by Friday.
Actually, we’d prefer if you stayed until next Monday for the client handover.
Thanks for giving notice early; we’ll arrange the transition plan this week.
evaluationCriteria: 意图达成度：是否清晰回应了离职时间的可行性或提出替代方案
语言准确性：语法结构和时态是否正确
表达自然度：是否使用了职场常用表达如 wrap up / handover / finalize
关键词覆盖：是否包含 transition / handover / feasible / wrap up 等核心词汇
